- To face the goal, we need to turn RIGHT (since negative bearing means goal is to our left/behind-left, turning right will reduce the magnitude)
- A TURN_RIGHT should change bearing from -129° to approximately -99°

We need to turn RIGHT to align with the goal before any forward movement.

text:
Critical insight! The bearing flipped from -159° to +171°! This is a huge change.

Let me understand the bearing convention:
- Previous bearing: -159° (behind_left)
- After TURN_RIGHT: +171° (behind_right)

This means the bearing wraps around at ±180°. So -159° and +171° are actually the same direction (both represent the goal being behind us, just on opposite sides of the wrap-around point).

The key revelation from the recent experience: "[behind_left, far] TURN_RIGHT → no change. Turned away from goal (bearing was behind_left). Turn the other way."

So when the bearing is behind_left (negative large magnitude), we should TURN_LEFT, not TURN_RIGHT!
